description: Language, date, number, or currency formatting is hardcoded for one locale, mis-serving users in other regions.
body: Failure mode: the template embeds English phrasing and one region's date/number/currency format, so users in other locales get mislocalized, sometimes wrong-meaning output, and the system assumes output language equals system language. Detection signals: no locale parameter; hardcoded format strings; output language fixed regardless of user; no cross-locale test. Do not bake one locale into the template. Safer fallback: take locale as an explicit input, select localized fragments, format dates/numbers/currency per locale, specify output language, and test that switching locale localizes correctly without changing instruction structure.
